input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10. This is a known change in .NET MAUI / Android behavior.

Let me provide a concise, helpful answer based on my knowledge of .NET MAUI changes.

In .NET 10 (and actually starting with .NET 9 updates), the `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated in Android API 30+ because Android moved to the new `WindowInsets` API. 

The `Resize` mode was deprecated at the Android platform level, so for .NET 10 the recommended approach is to use `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` as a cross-platform solution or handle it directly through Android's `WindowInsets` API for edge-to-edge display handling.

`KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` is MAUI's built-in auto-scroll behavior enabled by default, though layout structure matters—typically using a `Grid` with the input positioned at the bottom works best. For .NET 10 specifically, the edge-to-edge mode changes in Android are likely the underlying issue to account for.
